questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.65]\draw (-2,1)--(2,1);\draw (-2,-1)--(2,-1);\draw (-1.3,-1.8)--(0.8,1.8);\draw[->] (-1,1)--(-0.2,1);\draw[->] (-1,-1)--(-0.2,-1);\node at (0.85,1.3) {$67^{\circ}$};\node at (-0.35,-0.62) {$a$};\node at (-1.15,-0.6) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
An angle of $67^{\circ}$ corresponds to $a$. Angle $x$ is adjacent to $a$ on a straight line. Find $x$.

options.why: Transfers the corresponding angle but omits the straight-line step.
Subtracts from $90$ degrees instead of a straight line.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of a straight line.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.65]\draw (-2,1)--(2,1);\draw (-2,-1)--(2,-1);\draw (-1.3,-1.8)--(0.8,1.8);\draw[->] (-1,1)--(-0.2,1);\draw[->] (-1,-1)--(-0.2,-1);\node at (-0.2,1.3) {$124^{\circ}$};\node at (0.72,0.62) {$a$};\node at (-1.2,-0.6) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A $124^{\circ}$ angle is vertically opposite $a$, and $a$ is alternate to $x$ on parallel lines. Find $x$.

options.why: Treats the vertically opposite or alternate pair as supplementary.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of using the two equalities.
Halves the known angle instead of transferring it unchanged.
Transfers the angle but treats co-interior angles as equal.
Uses a $360$ degree total for a co-interior pair.
Subtracts the transferred angle from $174$ instead of $180$ degrees.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.65]\draw (-3,1)--(2.5,1);\draw (-3,-1)--(2.5,-1);\draw[->] (-1.2,1)--(-0.4,1);\draw[->] (-1.2,-1)--(-0.4,-1);\coordinate (O) at (0,-1);\draw (O)--(0.7,1);\draw (O)--(-2.56,1);\node at (0.6,-0.52) {$x$};\node at (-0.22,-0.33) {$71^{\circ}$};\node at (-0.9,-0.68) {$38^{\circ}$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Two transferred angles of $38^{\circ}$ and $71^{\circ}$ sit adjacent to $x$ on a straight line. Find $x$.

options.why: Adds the transferred angles but does not subtract from $180$ degrees.
Subtracts the transferred angles from a full turn.
Subtracts the larger transferred angle from the smaller result.
Subtracts the transferred angles instead of combining adjacent parts.
Subtracts the two angles from $180$ instead of adding them.
Finds the reflex angle instead of the requested smaller angle.
